nodes.title: Foreword by Thomas J. DiLorenzo
nodes.label: Foreword
nodes.description: Places the essay in the tradition of the Declaration of Independence and warns against modern forms of legal plunder.

nodes.title: Collective Force
nodes.label: Collective Force
nodes.description: Individuals may combine their right of defense into a regular common force.

nodes.title: Justice
nodes.label: Justice
nodes.description: Law's mission is to cause justice to reign by protecting rights equally.

nodes.title: Law as the Organization of Defense
nodes.label: Law Defined as Organized Defense
nodes.description: Law is the substitution of collective force for individual force for the sole purpose of protecting persons, liberties and property.

nodes.title: Lawful Defense
nodes.label: Lawful Defense
nodes.description: Every person has a natural right to defend life, liberty and property.

nodes.title: Natural Rights
nodes.label: Natural Rights
nodes.description: Life, liberty and property are God-given and exist prior to any legislation.

nodes.title: Non-Aggression
nodes.label: Non-Aggression
nodes.description: Force has not been given us to annihilate the equal rights of others.

nodes.title: Order and Stability
nodes.label: Order and Stability
nodes.description: A government that only protects produces the most stable and natural social order.

nodes.title: Personality, Liberty, and Property
nodes.label: Personality, Liberty, Property
nodes.description: The three constitutive elements of man.

nodes.title: France in 1850
nodes.label: France 1850
nodes.description: Revolution, national workshops, socialist agitation in the legislature.

nodes.title: The American Ideal
nodes.label: The American Ideal
nodes.description: Bastiat saw the early United States as the closest real-world approximation of proper law.

nodes.title: Slavery and Tariffs
nodes.label: Exceptions in America
nodes.description: Bastiat explicitly excepted these two evils from his praise of America.

nodes.title: Conclusion
nodes.label: Conclusion
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Conclusion.

nodes.title: Justice vs Law
nodes.label: Justice vs Law
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Justice vs Law.

nodes.title: Politics
nodes.label: Politics
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Politics.

nodes.title: Government Role
nodes.label: Government Role
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Government Role.

nodes.title: Law Definition
nodes.label: Law Definition
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Law Definition.

nodes.title: Causes
nodes.label: Causes
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Causes.

nodes.title: Origin of Plunder
nodes.label: Origin of Plunder
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Origin of Plunder.

nodes.title: Socialists
nodes.label: Socialists
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Socialists.

nodes.title: Suffrage
nodes.label: Suffrage
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Suffrage.

nodes.title: Three Gifts
nodes.label: Three Gifts
nodes.description: Direct quotation: Three Gifts.

nodes.title: Frédéric Bastiat
nodes.label: Frédéric Bastiat
nodes.description: 1801-1850. French economist, free-trader, and author of The Law and Economic Harmonies.

nodes.title: Thomas J. DiLorenzo
nodes.label: Thomas J. DiLorenzo
nodes.description: Economist, author of the foreword in the Mises Institute edition.

nodes.title: Class Conflict
nodes.label: Class Conflict
nodes.description: Every group organizes to plunder or to defend against plunder.

nodes.title: Corruption of Education
nodes.label: Corruption of Education
nodes.description: State-paid teachers will not criticize the laws that pay them.

nodes.title: Demoralization of Citizens
nodes.label: Demoralization
nodes.description: When law commands injustice citizens become cynics or accomplices.

nodes.title: Economic Impoverishment
nodes.label: Economic Impoverishment
nodes.description: Legal plunder reduces total wealth even for many of its beneficiaries.

nodes.title: Effacing Justice
nodes.label: Effacing Justice
nodes.description: Law that sanctions plunder destroys the distinction between justice and injustice.

nodes.title: Instability and Revolution
nodes.label: Instability
nodes.description: Generalized plunder produces constant pressure for upheaval.

nodes.title: Loss of Moral Sense
nodes.label: Loss of Moral Sense
nodes.description: Citizens must choose between conscience and respect for law.

nodes.title: Politicization of Society
nodes.label: Politicization
nodes.description: When law = plunder, politics becomes the main arena of life.

nodes.title: Rent Seeking
nodes.label: Rent Seeking
nodes.description: Resources spent obtaining political transfers rather than producing value.

nodes.title: Legal Plunder
nodes.label: legal plunder
nodes.description: Law used to take the property of some and give it to others without consent.

nodes.title: Political Economy of Tariffs
nodes.label: Political Economy
nodes.description: Concentrated interests lobby; consumers stay ignorant.

nodes.title: Tariff Effects on Consumers
nodes.label: Effects on Consumers
nodes.description: Consumers pay the hidden tax of protection.

nodes.title: Forced Labor
nodes.label: Forced Labor
nodes.description: Compulsory service extracted by the state.

nodes.title: Government Jobs Programs
nodes.label: Government Jobs Programs
nodes.description: Make-work at taxpayer expense.

nodes.title: Minimum Wage Laws
nodes.label: Minimum Wage Laws
nodes.description: Price floor on labor that creates unemployment.

nodes.title: Monopolies
nodes.label: Monopolies
nodes.description: State-granted exclusive privileges.

nodes.title: Progressive Taxation
nodes.label: Progressive Taxation
nodes.description: Using the tax code to redistribute.

nodes.title: Protectionism & Tariffs
nodes.label: Protectionism & Tariffs
nodes.description: Tariffs force consumers to subsidize producers.

nodes.title: Public Schools
nodes.label: Public Schools
nodes.description: Tax funded schooling used for indoctrination.

nodes.title: Subsidies
nodes.label: Subsidies
nodes.description: Tax money given to favored groups.

nodes.title: Usury Laws
nodes.label: Usury Laws
nodes.description: Interest caps that reduce credit for the poor.

nodes.title: Welfare
nodes.label: Welfare
nodes.description: Organized transfer presented as the poor's right to plunder.

nodes.title: Universal Plunder
nodes.label: Universal Plunder
nodes.description: When the principle is accepted, plunder becomes the equilibrium state of politics.

nodes.title: Contemporary Legal Plunder
nodes.label: Contemporary Legal Plunder
nodes.description: Tariffs, subsidies, entitlements, regulatory capture, occupational licensing all fit the pattern.

nodes.title: Influence on Austrian Economics
nodes.label: Influence on Austrian Economics
nodes.description: Precursor emphasis on spontaneous order and critique of intervention.

nodes.title: Influence on Libertarianism
nodes.label: Influence on Libertarianism
nodes.description: Foundational text for natural-rights and limited-government thought.

nodes.title: Lessons for Today
nodes.label: Lessons for Today
nodes.description: Confine law to defense. Reject the fatal principle that law may take from one to give to another.

nodes.title: Rent Seeking Today
nodes.label: Rent Seeking Today
nodes.description: Industries and interest groups compete for favors instead of serving customers.

nodes.title: Causes of Perversion
nodes.label: Causes
nodes.description: Naked greed and misconceived philanthropy.

nodes.title: False Philanthropy
nodes.label: False Philanthropy
nodes.description: The desire to do good to some by robbing others via law.

nodes.title: Greed
nodes.label: Greed
nodes.description: Legislators using power to live at others' expense.

nodes.title: The Law Perverted
nodes.label: The Law Perverted
nodes.description: Law diverted from protection to predation.

nodes.title: Law as Plunder Instrument
nodes.label: Law as Instrument of Plunder
nodes.description: Law protects plunderers instead of the plundered.

nodes.title: Law Manufacturing
nodes.label: Law Manufacturing
nodes.description: Plundered classes try to enter the business of making law.

nodes.title: Political Struggle
nodes.label: Political Struggle
nodes.description: Politics receives exaggerated importance precisely because it controls plunder.

nodes.title: Universal Suffrage
nodes.label: Universal Suffrage
nodes.description: When law can plunder, the fight over who may vote becomes ferocious.

nodes.title: Free Trade
nodes.label: Free Trade
nodes.description: Exchange is production. Barriers are plunder.

nodes.title: Individual Rights
nodes.label: Individual Rights
nodes.description: The moral unit is the individual; collective force exists only to protect individuals.

nodes.title: Limited vs Activist State
nodes.label: Limited vs Activist State
nodes.description: The activist state that tries to organize everything becomes the central plunderer.

nodes.title: Natural Law
nodes.label: Natural Law
nodes.description: Rights inhere in human nature; positive law is valid only insofar as it recognizes them.

nodes.title: Voluntary vs Coerced
nodes.label: Voluntary Philanthropy
nodes.description: True giving requires a willing giver. Coercion destroys the moral act.

nodes.title: Property Rights
nodes.label: Property Rights
nodes.description: Property is the extension of faculties. Attacks on property are attacks on liberty and personality.

nodes.title: Rule of Law
nodes.label: Rule of Law
nodes.description: Law must be general and equal. Targeting specific groups for plunder violates the rule of law.

nodes.title: Spontaneous Order
nodes.label: Spontaneous Order
nodes.description: When law stays within its sphere, order emerges naturally.

nodes.title: Critique of Coercive Charity
nodes.label: Critique of Coercive Charity
nodes.description: Taking by taxation is not charity.

nodes.title: Education as Indoctrination
nodes.label: Education as Indoctrination
nodes.description: State schools serve the state that funds them.

nodes.title: Forced Conformity
nodes.label: Forced Conformity
nodes.description: Regulation and regimentation imposed on the whole population.

nodes.title: Playing God
nodes.label: Playing God
nodes.description: Socialists want to remake man and society according to their plan.

nodes.title: Socialist Doctrine
nodes.label: Socialist Doctrine
nodes.description: The program of systematized legal plunder under philanthropic slogans.

nodes.title: The Law
nodes.label: The Law
nodes.description: Bastiat's treatise: law exists to protect natural rights; when turned into plunder it destroys justice and liberty.

nodes.title: Limited Government
nodes.label: Limited Government
nodes.description: The most restrained government is the most just and stable.

nodes.title: Negative Role of Law
nodes.label: Negative Role
nodes.description: Law represses injustice; it does not positively organize society.

nodes.title: Non-Intervention
nodes.label: Non-Intervention
nodes.description: Wants and satisfactions develop in their natural order.

nodes.title: Proper Sphere of Law
nodes.label: Proper Sphere
nodes.description: Law confined to protecting persons, liberties and properties.

nodes.title: Protection of Rights
nodes.label: Protection of Rights
nodes.description: The only legitimate end of collective force.
